```{python}
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns 
```

```{python}
data = pd.read_csv("/Users/shubhantamhane/nfl-qb-archetypes/code/data_with_epa.csv")
```

Imputation from code before
```{python}
data['lng_rush'] = data['lng_rush'].fillna(data['lng_rush'].mean())
data['qbr'] = data['qbr'].fillna(data['qbr'].mean())
```

Scaling and other preprocessing 
```{python}
scaler = StandardScaler()

numeric_data = data.select_dtypes(include=['float64', 'int64'])

numeric_data = numeric_data.drop(columns=['year'])
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(numeric_data)
```

Importing PCA
```{python}
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA

pca = PCA(n_components=None)
X_pca = pca.fit_transform(X_scaled)
```

Scree plot. Seems to be about 12 PCS fro 90% of variance
```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
plt.plot(range(1, len(pca.explained_variance_ratio_)+1),
         pca.explained_variance_ratio_.cumsum(),
         marker='o')
plt.axhline(y=0.9, color='r', linestyle='--')
plt.xlabel("Number of Principal Components")
plt.ylabel("Cumulative Explained Variance")
plt.title("PCA Explained Variance")
plt.show()
```

13 PCs for 90% of variance 
```{python}
pca = PCA(n_components=0.9)
pca.fit(X_scaled)
print(pca.n_components_)
```


Code to create loading matrix. Asks the question: Which features are most prevalent in determining principal components? 
```{python}
loading_matrix = pd.DataFrame(
    pca.components_.T,
    index=numeric_data.columns,
    columns=[f'PC{i+1}' for i in range(pca.n_components_)]
)

print(loading_matrix.round(3))
```

Heatmap for easier visualization
```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 10))
sns.heatmap(
    loading_matrix,
    annot=True,
    fmt=".2f",
    cmap='coolwarm',
    center=0,
    annot_kws={"size": 7},
    linewidths=0.5
)
plt.title("PCA Loading Matrix", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Principal Component")
plt.ylabel("Feature")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

Scree plot for eigenvalues and proportion of variance explained by PC. 
```{python}
eigenvalues = pca.explained_variance_[:13]
var_ratio = pca.explained_variance_ratio_[:13]
pc_labels = [f'PC{i+1}' for i in range(13)]

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

ax1.bar(pc_labels, var_ratio, color='steelblue', alpha=0.7, label='% Variance Explained')
ax1.set_ylabel('Proportion of Variance Explained', color='steelblue')
ax1.set_ylim(0, max(var_ratio) * 1.3)

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.plot(pc_labels, eigenvalues, color='red', marker='o', linewidth=2, label='Eigenvalue')
ax2.axhline(y=1, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='Eigenvalue = 1 (Kaiser)')
ax2.set_ylabel('Eigenvalue', color='red')

lines1, labels1 = ax1.get_legend_handles_labels()
lines2, labels2 = ax2.get_legend_handles_labels()
ax1.legend(lines1 + lines2, labels1 + labels2, loc='upper right')

plt.title('Scree Plot —  Principal Components')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
pc_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    'PC': [f'PC{i+1}' for i in range(pca.n_components_)],
    'Eigenvalue': pca.explained_variance_.round(3),
    'Variance Explained (%)': (pca.explained_variance_ratio_ * 100).round(2)
})

print(pc_summary.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
X_clustered = X_pca

from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score

K_range = range(2,11)

sil_scores = []

for k in K_range:
    km = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    labels = km.fit_predict(X_clustered)
    score = silhouette_score(X_clustered, labels)
    sil_scores.append(score)

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
plt.plot(K_range, sil_scores, marker='o')
plt.xlabel("Number of clusters (k)")
plt.ylabel("Silhouette score")
plt.title("Silhouette Score vs k")
plt.xticks(K_range)
plt.show()

list(zip(K_range, sil_scores))
```
